column2: Any document, register, workbook, or record produced under the Theragen documentation standard.
An approved version of an artifact; subsequent changes require a Change Request.
PostgreSQL auto-incrementing 8-byte integer used for monotonic IDs (audit trails).
Corrective and Preventive Action — formal record opened in response to deviation or finding.
PostgreSQL enumerated type. Values listed on the Enumerations sheets.
A compliance frame — a regulation or standard that governs records.
INT-YYYY-NNNN — issued by triage; propagates downstream into every artifact.
PostgreSQL type for IP addresses, used in audit-trail and access records.
PostgreSQL binary JSON type. Used for multi-select stores and audit before/after states.
Marking a row inactive (e.g. via active=FALSE) rather than DELETE'ing it. Preferred for audit-bearing entities.
Universally unique identifier (v4). Used as surrogate primary key throughout.
Work Breakdown Structure — hierarchical decomposition of project work.
